themes.title: Far-right parties push 'national priority' policies
themes.blurb: Right-wing and far-right parties across Europe are intensifying their focus on 'national priority' policies, which prioritize citizens over immigrants in access to social benefits, housing, and employment. This theme is particularly prominent in Spain, France, and Italy, where parties like Vox, Rassemblement National, and Lega are campaigning on these issues ahead of elections.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 Vox
countries.note: Vox campaigns in Andalusia on 'national priority' policies, opposing immigrant regularization and demanding 'Spaniards first' in social aid.

countries.name: 🇫🇷 Rassemblement National
countries.note: Jordan Bardella proposes mandatory school uniforms and respectful address, emphasizing national identity and cultural cohesion.

countries.name: 🇮🇹 Fratelli d'Italia / Lega
countries.note: Lega and Fratelli d'Italia push for housing policies favoring Italian citizens, with Salvini criticizing EU migration policies.

countries.name: 🇬🇧 Reform UK
countries.note: Reform UK advocates for stricter immigration controls and prioritizing British citizens in public services and housing.

countries.name: 🇳🇴 Fremskrittspartiet
countries.note: FrP emphasizes national sovereignty and stricter immigration policies, aligning with 'national priority' rhetoric.

themes.title: Trump’s policies dominate global right-wing coverage
themes.blurb: Donald Trump's domestic and foreign policies continue to shape right-wing discourse worldwide, with his administration's actions on tariffs, Iran, and immigration resonating in Europe. Right-wing parties in the UK, Spain, and Italy frequently reference Trump’s strategies, either in support or opposition, highlighting his enduring influence on transnational right-wing politics.

countries.name: 🇺🇸 MAGA / Trump
countries.note: Trump's tariffs on EU cars, Iran war policies, and immigration crackdowns are central to his administration's agenda.

countries.name: 🇬🇧 Reform UK
countries.note: Reform UK echoes Trump’s anti-immigration rhetoric and economic nationalism, positioning itself as a UK alternative to mainstream conservatism.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 Vox
countries.note: Vox opposes the EU-Mercosur trade deal, citing Trump-like concerns about national sovereignty and economic protectionism.

countries.name: 🇮🇹 Fratelli d'Italia / Lega
countries.note: Meloni and Salvini reference Trump’s foreign policy, particularly on Iran and migration, while advocating for Italy-first economic policies.

countries.name: 🇵🇱 Prawo i Sprawiedliwość
countries.note: PiS aligns with Trump’s stance on NATO and U.S. relations, emphasizing sovereignty and opposition to EU overreach.

themes.title: Far-right parties surge in European polls
themes.blurb: Far-right parties across Europe are experiencing a surge in polling numbers, driven by voter dissatisfaction with mainstream parties and concerns over immigration, economic instability, and national identity. This trend is evident in Germany, France, Sweden, and the UK, where parties like AfD, RN, Sverigedemokraterna, and Reform UK are gaining ground ahead of upcoming elections.

countries.name: 🇩🇪 Alternative für Deutschland
countries.note: AfD leads polls in several German states, capitalizing on anti-immigration sentiment and economic discontent.

countries.name: 🇫🇷 Rassemblement National
countries.note: RN gains in French polls, with Marine Le Pen and Jordan Bardella positioning the party as a viable alternative to Macron.

countries.name: 🇸🇪 Sverigedemokraterna
countries.note: Sverigedemokraterna disrupts Sweden’s political system, pushing for stricter immigration policies and national sovereignty.

countries.name: 🇬🇧 Reform UK
countries.note: Reform UK polls higher than the Conservatives in some UK regions, appealing to voters disillusioned with mainstream parties.

countries.name: 🇳🇴 Fremskrittspartiet
countries.note: FrP achieves record poll numbers in Norway, focusing on lower taxes and stricter immigration controls.

themes.title: Hungary’s political shift under new leadership
themes.blurb: Hungary’s political landscape is undergoing a significant transformation following the election of Péter Magyar’s Tisza Party, which defeated Viktor Orbán’s Fidesz. The new government is navigating challenges related to EU relations, corruption allegations, and domestic reforms, while neighboring countries and European allies closely monitor its policies.

countries.name: 🇭🇺 Fidesz / Tisza
countries.note: Tisza Party forms a new government after defeating Fidesz, focusing on anti-corruption and EU relations.

countries.name: 🇵🇱 Prawo i Sprawiedliwość
countries.note: PiS reacts to Hungary’s political shift, emphasizing shared conservative values and concerns over EU influence.

countries.name: 🇩🇪 Alternative für Deutschland
countries.note: AfD monitors Hungary’s political developments, drawing parallels with its own anti-EU and anti-immigration stance.

themes.title: Far-right rhetoric fuels political violence
themes.blurb: Far-right rhetoric in Europe and the U.S. is increasingly linked to incidents of political violence, with leaders’ inflammatory language contributing to a polarized and volatile political climate. This trend is evident in Italy, the UK, and the U.S., where far-right figures and their supporters have been involved in confrontations, threats, and acts of intimidation.

countries.name: 🇮🇹 Fratelli d'Italia / Lega
countries.note: Far-right rhetoric in Italy fuels protests and clashes, particularly around immigration and labor policies.

countries.name: 🇬🇧 Reform UK
countries.note: Reform UK candidates face allegations of inciting violence and spreading hate speech, contributing to a tense political atmosphere.

countries.name: 🇺🇸 MAGA / Trump
countries.note: Trump’s rhetoric is linked to political violence, including assassination attempts and threats against opponents.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 Vox
countries.note: Vox’s inflammatory language against immigrants and political opponents sparks protests and legal challenges.
